p.strong: Technical Specification for Zero-Copy Binary Wire Format

g.text: Scenario 1: Forward Compatibility (Old Code + New Data)
Scenario 2: Backward Compatibility (New Code + Old Data)

div.p: FlatBuffer field IDs are immutable. Once a field is assigned an index in the VTable, that index belongs to that field forever. Changing the type or re-using the ID is a catastrophic failure in zero-copy systems.

g.text: T ReadField(const Table *table, voffset_t field_id, T default_val) {

g.text: return default_val; // Field added in newer schema
return default_val; // Field was explicitly set to null/default

text.tspan: // 2. Check if field_id is within this specific buffer's vtable
// Field was explicitly set to null/default

ul.li: Field 3 is simply ignored by the older binary.
VTable size is only 12. Offset 10 exists but value is checked.
If ID was totally missing, size check 10 < vtable_size might fail if older.
Reader detects missing field -> returns schema default (e.g., 0).

g.text: Reader interprets 4 bytes as 8 -> Offset Shift + Memory Corruption
Old reader gets timestamp and thinks it is a price value.
